title: Plan a reindex for any mapping or analyzer change
description: Treat analyzer and most field-type changes as requiring a full reindex via an alias, since they cannot apply to already-indexed documents.
body: Use when changing an analyzer, field type, or most mapping settings. Do not expect a mapping change to retroactively affect existing documents — they were tokenized under the old analyzer and keep the old terms until reindexed. Actions: create a new index with the updated mapping, reindex into it, and swap a read alias atomically for zero-downtime cutover; keep the old index until verified. Validation signals include the alias pointing at the new index and queries returning consistent results post-swap. Risk boundary: changing mappings in place produces a half-old/half-new index with inconsistent results; require a reindex-via-alias plan before any analyzer/type change.
